what_it_does: Implements the vi-vision:// URI bridge for the company runtime: it parses addresses, resolves visual-dev component records from a Supabase-backed registry using anonymous reads with scope cascading, writes generated assets via a dedicated service-account principal, and exposes a read-only catalog palette.
observations: Fail-loud custom exceptions are used throughout; silent empty returns are explicitly rejected.
Reads and writes use separate auth principals: anonymous key for reads, service-account token for writes.
Scope resolution follows a most-specific-wins cascade: global < project < user < session.
Writes use a select-then-write upsert pattern: PATCH an existing owned row or INSERT a new one.
RLS enforcement is delegated to existing Supabase policies rather than app-level checks.
Environment variables drive configuration with fallback names.
Token caching and near-expiry refresh are implemented for the service account.
Pure logic functions accept injectable fetcher/poster callbacks for offline unit testing.
The module includes an operational self-test under __main__.
The catalog returns human-friendly name/type/description summaries, not raw ids.
standouts: Explicit read/write auth principal split inside one bridge module.
Most-specific-wins scope cascade implemented in pure Python.
Service-account session/refresh token flow with local cache.
Select-then-write upsert mirroring a separate component-store.js implementation.
conventions: Fail-loud with domain-specific exceptions rather than returning None or empty.
Least-privilege auth: read path never uses the write-capable principal.
Dependency injection hooks for network functions to enable backend-free tests.
Environment-driven configuration with prefixed primary vars and unprefixed fallbacks.
Docstring-heavy, operational rather than happy-path focus.
RLS treated as the source of truth for access control.
concerns: _candidates_http silently returns [] when no VI_VISION_RESOLVE_URL is set, which can mask a missing transport if Supabase is also unconfigured.
_transport_configured only checks environment variable presence, not actual connectivity.
write_vi_vision contains a dead branch: existing = _candidates_http(...) if False else None.
build_write_row raises ViVisionAddressError for a non-address reason (empty definition).
jwt_sub decodes the JWT payload without signature verification, acknowledged but still a local assumption.
notes: A fail-loud, auth-split bridge that resolves and writes vi-vision:// component assets against a Supabase registry with scope cascading.
questions: What is the exact schema of the visual_dev_component_registry table, including the COALESCE-indexed scope column?
How is the service-account user created and granted the RLS role needed for company writes?
How are assets promoted from project/user scope to the global shared library?
What response format does VI_VISION_RESOLVE_URL expect?
How does this module relate concretely to runtime/cognition.py:resolve_address?
What does the runtime scope kind represent, and is it actually stored in the registry?
intention_signals: To provide a secure, auditable, offline-testable bridge between the company spine and a shared/private Supabase component registry, ensuring reads never carry write credentials and writes are gated to a dedicated revocable service account.
novelty: The explicit read/write principal fork within one module, combined with a pure-Python scope-cascade selector and a service-account token cache, makes this a self-contained policy-aware bridge rather than a thin API wrapper.
contribution: The vi-vision address parser, scope-cascade resolver, read/write auth split, service-account token manager, select-then-write upsert builder, and read-only catalog formatter.
summary_for_embedding: A Python bridge module that parses vi-vision:// addresses, resolves visual-dev components from a Supabase registry with scope cascading via anonymous reads, and writes generated assets via a dedicated service-account principal.
intention_for_embedding: To give the company runtime a fail-loud, least-privilege, testable interface to a shared/private factory component library.

symbols.does: Custom exception for malformed vi-vision addresses.
symbols.description: Raised when a vi-vision:// string is missing the scheme, has an unknown frame, lacks required ids, uses an invalid asset type, or contains slashes in the asset id.

symbols.does: Custom exception when a parsed address resolves no asset.
symbols.description: Raised by resolve_vi_vision when no candidate row matches the requested type and scope cascade, ensuring reads never silently return empty.

symbols.does: Custom exception for network or store failures.
symbols.description: Raised when HTTP requests to the read endpoint or Supabase REST API fail or return non-JSON, preserving the fail-loud contract.

symbols.does: Custom exception for service-account authentication failures.
symbols.description: Raised when SA credentials are missing, the token endpoint returns an error, or the access token lacks a sub claim.

symbols.does: Parses a vi-vision:// URI into frame, scope, type, and id.
symbols.description: Validates the address grammar global|project|user|session/<type>/<id>, rejects unknown frames and types, and returns a dictionary used by resolve and write paths.

symbols.does: Extracts the scope kind from a registry row.
symbols.description: Normalizes the row's scope field whether stored as a string, a dict with kind/scope keys, or something else, defaulting to global.

symbols.does: Picks the most-specific matching row within the requested frame.
symbols.description: Iterates candidate rows, filters by type, ignores rows more specific than the requested frame, and returns the highest-rank match using the global/project/user/session/runtime ordering.

symbols.does: Decodes a URL-safe base64 JSON segment.
symbols.description: Adds padding as needed and parses the segment with json.loads; used to inspect JWT payload claims without verifying the signature.

symbols.does: Returns the sub claim from an access token payload.
symbols.description: Splits the JWT, base64-decodes the payload segment, and returns the sub value as the principal uid; raises ViVisionAuthError on malformed tokens.

symbols.does: Reads the Supabase project URL from environment.
symbols.description: Prefers VI_VISION_SUPABASE_URL and falls back to SUPABASE_URL.

symbols.does: Reads the Supabase anon key from environment.
symbols.description: Prefers VI_VISION_ANON_KEY and falls back to SUPABASE_ANON_KEY; used as the PostgREST apikey and anon Bearer token.

symbols.does: Performs an HTTP POST and returns status plus body text.
symbols.description: Serializes the payload to JSON, sends the request with urllib, and returns either the success response or the HTTPError status and body.

symbols.does: Caches and refreshes the service-account access token.
symbols.description: Uses password or refresh_token grant against Supabase auth, caches the token until near expiry, and returns the Bearer token for write requests.

symbols.does: Builds anonymous read-only request headers.
symbols.description: Returns apikey plus Authorization Bearer set to the anon key, so reads operate under the anon RLS role and never use the SA principal.

symbols.does: Builds write-capable request headers for the SA principal.
symbols.description: Returns apikey set to anon and Authorization Bearer set to the cached SA access token; raises if no SA credentials are configured.

symbols.does: Fetches candidate rows from an optional read-only HTTP endpoint.
symbols.description: Calls VI_VISION_RESOLVE_URL with a component_id query parameter and returns parsed JSON rows; raises on transport or parse errors.

symbols.does: Fetches candidate rows from Supabase REST for a component id.
symbols.description: Queries the visual_dev_component_registry table with anon read headers and returns the parsed row list.

symbols.does: Checks whether any read transport is configured.
symbols.description: Returns true if VI_VISION_RESOLVE_URL or a Supabase URL is set; used before attempting resolution.

symbols.does: Resolves a vi-vision:// address to a registry record.
symbols.description: Parses the address, fetches candidates via HTTP or Supabase, applies select_scoped, and raises ViVisionNotFound if no match exists.

symbols.does: Constructs the registry row for a write without touching the backend.
symbols.description: Parses the address, validates the definition, and builds a row with component_id, type, scope, user_id, project_id when applicable, and derived name/tags/context.

symbols.does: Upserts a generated asset into the registry as the SA principal.
symbols.description: Builds the row, selects an existing owned row by component_id and scope, PATCHes it with a bumped version if found, otherwise POSTs a new row, and returns the persisted record.

symbols.does: Performs an HTTP PATCH and returns status plus body text.
symbols.description: Serializes the payload and sends a PATCH request with Prefer return=representation; used by write_vi_vision for upsert updates.

symbols.does: Returns a read-only palette summary for a scope.
symbols.description: Queries the registry for rows matching the frame's scope kind and returns a compact list of component_id, type, name, tags, and a truncated description for brain context enrichment.
